ComparePayload.description:
Inputs for ``experiment compare`` in ``--reuse-runs`` mode.

Each ``run_ids[i]`` must resolve to a completed run dir under the
server's ``store_root``. The webapp passes each run's existing
``config.yaml`` to the CLI's ``--config`` flag in matching order.
From-scratch compare (no ``--reuse-runs``) is intentionally out of
scope here - heavy and rarely interactive.
ComparePayload.title: ComparePayload

DeploymentCreate.description:
POST /deployments body.

``name`` defaults to ``"<ticker>-<strategy>-<train_end>"`` and
``warmup_bars`` defaults to the strategy's
``required_warmup_bars + convergence_margin_bars`` (subject to an
absolute floor) when omitted.
DeploymentCreate.title: DeploymentCreate

DeploymentDetail.description: Summary + the tail of the signal log.
DeploymentDetail.title: DeploymentDetail

DeploymentSummary.description: Row in the deployments list view.
DeploymentSummary.title: DeploymentSummary

FeatureImportanceEntry.description:
One feature's cross-fold aggregated importance under one method.

``importance`` and ``std`` are ``None`` when the aggregate is non-finite
(a degenerate fold the driver could not score).
FeatureImportanceEntry.title: FeatureImportanceEntry

FeatureImportanceResponse.description:
Cross-fold feature importance for `/api/runs/{experiment_id}/feature-importance`.

``entries`` is empty and ``message`` is set for the common "no artifact"
cases (importance not requested for the run, a rule-based strategy that
emits none, or a pre-importance run) so the endpoint stays 200.

``computable`` is whether this run's strategy can produce importance at all
(feature-consuming strategies can; rule-based ones cannot). The frontend
offers an on-demand "compute importance" action only when ``computable`` is
true and ``entries`` is empty.

``diverged_run_id`` is set when a prior recompute diverged (the re-fit
didn't reproduce this run's metrics) and saved importance as a separate
run; the detail page links to it persistently across reloads.
FeatureImportanceResponse.title: FeatureImportanceResponse

HoldoutPayload.description:
Inputs for ``experiment holdout-eval`` (run-dir XOR hpo-best source).

``source_id`` is interpreted as a run id when ``source_kind='run'``
and as an HPO study name when ``source_kind='hpo'``. The job_service
resolves it to an on-disk path and surfaces a 422 with structured
``loc`` if the source is missing the required artifacts (a non-null
``holdout_start`` for runs, ``best_config.yaml`` for HPO studies).
HoldoutPayload.title: HoldoutPayload

ImportanceMethod.description: How a feature-importance score was produced.
ImportanceMethod.title: ImportanceMethod

ImportancePayload.description:
Input for ``experiment importance``: the finished run to compute for.

``run_id`` must resolve to a completed run dir under the server's
store_root whose strategy consumes engineered features and that does not
already carry feature importance. The job_service surfaces a 422 with a
structured ``loc`` when those preconditions fail.
ImportancePayload.title: ImportancePayload

Interval.description: Bar timeframe - used for annualization factor computation.
Interval.title: Interval

JobKind.description:
Kind of framework operation that produces a persisted artifact.

Every ``experiment`` CLI subcommand emits one of these. Defined in core
so the CLI and the webapp share a single definition (the webapp uses it
as a job's kind); the CLI must not import webapp to name the kinds.
JobKind.title: JobKind

LegStep.description: The three sub-steps of a study leg, in canonical execution order.
LegStep.title: LegStep

ParamImportanceResponse.description:
Per-hyperparameter relative importance for the live HPO monitor.

``importance`` is empty and ``message`` is set when the study has too few
completed trials, the optuna DB is missing, or the importance evaluator
raises on a degenerate search space - the endpoint stays 200 so the
frontend renders an empty-state card instead of erroring.
ParamImportanceResponse.title: ParamImportanceResponse

PredictIfStaleResponse.description:
Response for POST /deployments/{id}/predict-if-stale.

``stale=False`` means the cached signal already covered the latest
available bar; ``stale=True`` means a fresh predict ran.
PredictIfStaleResponse.title: PredictIfStaleResponse

RunSummary.description:
List-view row for `/api/runs`.

Strategy/tickers/interval are sourced from the run's frozen
`config.yaml` so any future strategy works without changing this code.
``has_holdout`` mirrors ``manifest.holdout_start is not None`` so the
holdout-launcher form can filter eligible runs without a per-row
detail fetch. ``data_hash`` is the run's bar-series fingerprint so the
compare picker can lock subsequent selections to the first run's
universe (the paired-bootstrap test rejects mixed bar indices).
RunSummary.title: RunSummary

ScoredSignalOut.description:
One emitted signal scored open->open against realised session opens.

Entered at ``entry_date``'s open (the first session after ``bar_ts``),
exited at ``exit_date``'s open (the next session). ``listened_return``
is the signed, leverage-scaled realised return. The realised fields are
populated together iff ``scored`` is true - a signal stays unscored
until its exit session has opened. ``hit`` is null for a FLAT signal.
ScoredSignalOut.title: ScoredSignalOut

SignalEvaluationOut.description:
Response for GET /deployments/{id}/signal-evaluation.

Per-signal scores plus headline stats over the scored subset.
``hit_rate`` covers directional (non-FLAT) scored signals only;
``cumulative_return`` compounds every scored ``listened_return``
(gross), and ``net_cumulative_return`` does so after costs. All summary
stats are null when nothing has been scored yet. ``cost_scenario`` is
the tier whose slippage + commission produced the net figures.
SignalEvaluationOut.title: SignalEvaluationOut

SignalRowOut.description:
One row from the deployment's signal log.

``bar_ts`` is the last completed bar the signal was computed from;
``signal_date`` is the trading day the signal is *for* (the next
session after ``bar_ts``).
SignalRowOut.title: SignalRowOut

StudyConsolidatedDTO.description: Read view of a study's consolidated report (tables + plots tree).
StudyConsolidatedDTO.title: StudyConsolidatedDTO

StudyDirection.description:
Optimization direction surfaced from the Optuna study.

The framework hardcodes ``maximize`` (loss-style metrics negate at
the objective layer); this enum exists so the chart layer can pick
cummax vs cummin without re-reading the optuna sqlite per request.
StudyDirection.title: StudyDirection

StudyPayload.description:
Inputs for ``experiment study run`` (cross-strategy x cross-universe sweep).

``spec_name`` resolves to ``config/study/<spec_name>.yaml``; the
job_service parses it via ``StudySpec.model_validate`` and surfaces a
422 with structured ``loc`` on schema errors. ``only_legs`` entries
are validated against the spec's expanded leg ids in the handler.
StudyPayload.title: StudyPayload

StudySpecUploadDetail.description: Single-upload read shape with the full YAML body.
StudySpecUploadDetail.title: StudySpecUploadDetail

StudySpecUploadSummary.description: Listing entry - no YAML body, no validation errors.
StudySpecUploadSummary.title: StudySpecUploadSummary

StudySpecValidateRequest.description:
``POST /configs/study_spec/validate`` body - raw YAML text.

A dedicated wire type keeps the existing ValidateRequest (parsed payload)
uncoupled from the YAML-text path, which has to handle parse errors before
Pydantic ever sees the body.
StudySpecValidateRequest.title: StudySpecValidateRequest

UniverseSpecUploadDetail.description: Single-upload read shape with the full YAML body.
UniverseSpecUploadDetail.title: UniverseSpecUploadDetail

post.description: Recall today's signal or compute it now.

get.description: Score the deployment's emitted signals open->open; ``cost`` sets the net friction tier.

post.description:
Build (or rebuild) the consolidated report for a study and return it.

Runs the matplotlib + table-writing work in the threadpool so the event
loop stays responsive while the (few-seconds) job completes. A study
that hasn't completed any legs yet returns 422.

schema.title: Response Get Users Api Users Get
